title: She’s running the Boston Marathon for her former patient with Morquio syndrome
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Erin Donnelly is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in memory of her former patient Giovanni Thompson.
Giovanni Thompson, who had Morquio syndrome, was a patient at Franciscan Children's under Donnelly's care.
Thompson passed away at age 5, and Donnelly's marathon run honors his inspiring spirit.

title: Bruins fall to Devils 4-3 in OT as US Olympic hero Jack Hughes has 3 assists
author: The Associated Press
bullets: Bruins lose 4-3 in overtime to the Devils, impacting their playoff chances in the Eastern Conference.
Jack Hughes of the Devils had three assists, contributing to key goals including the game-winner by Paul Cotter.
David Pastrnak scored twice for Boston, but the Bruins couldn't secure back-to-back wins since late January.

title: Tyngsborough man charged with murder after human remains found buried under garage floor, police say
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Shawn Sullivan, 40, from Tyngsborough, charged with murder after remains found under his garage, believed to be missing Lowell woman.
Jill T. Kloppenburg was reported missing in February 2025; last seen in Lowell on January 2, 2025.
Investigators using radar found patched garage floor hiding human remains bag; cause of death under investigation.

title: Why a Texas mom is running the Boston Marathon for her son — and his MGH doctor
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Adrienne Felterman from Houston is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor her son and his doctor.
Her son, Charles, has Dup15q syndrome, and receives care from Dr. Ronald Thibert at Massachusetts General Hospital.
Felterman aims to raise money for MGH, grateful for the care her son has received over 14 years.

title: ‘She is truly a superhero’: Why a Belmont athlete is running Boston for his wife
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Ben Flowers, 35, from Belmont, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for his wife, Coney, a leukemia survivor.
Coney Flowers was diagnosed with Acute Lymphoblastic Leukemia in 2024 and underwent a successful bone marrow transplant.
Ben is raising funds for Caring for a Cure, an organization that supported them during Coney's treatment.

title: She’s running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support kids with cancer
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Kathleen Labrie will run the 2026 Boston Marathon for Massachusetts General Hospital's Pediatric Cancer team.
Labrie, 33, from Southampton, runs to honor children with cancer and invites donors to select honorees.
Her participation in the marathon signifies a personal and community dedication to pediatric cancer awareness.

title: Noah Kahan announces premiere date for documentary coming to Netflix
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Noah Kahan's documentary "Out of Body" premieres on Netflix on April 13.
Kahan's documentary details his creative journey, mental health, and family life over a year and a half.
Noah Kahan's fourth studio album, "The Great Divide," will be released on April 24.

title: Jon Keller, longtime WBZ political analyst, returns to TV
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Jon Keller returns to WBZ as a 'special contributor' after being laid off last fall.
Keller will resume political analysis, moderate major debates, and host 'Keller At Large'.
The 'Keller At Large' show returns Sunday morning with Gov. Maura Healey as a guest.

title: ‘A relic of a bygone era’: Here’s why readers want Massachusetts to stop changing its clocks
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts is considering legislation to end biannual clock changes, supported by Senate President Karen E. Spilka and Sen. John F. Keenan.
The bill proposes adopting permanent Atlantic Standard Time if two or more New England states agree to the same system.
83% of 470 readers in a Boston.com poll favored stopping clock changes due to disruptions and health concerns.

title: Impaired Stoneham teen drove 118 mph, fled police, and then crashed, N.H. authorities say
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A Stoneham teen was arrested after driving 118 mph and crashing into a wall in New Hampshire.
The 18-year-old driver fled police, traveling through several towns before crashing at a Route 101 overpass.
He faces multiple charges, including reckless conduct and aggravated DUI, and was held at Rockingham County Jail.
